publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:40:56 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM07861Hoshang Merchant papers7861Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:06:11 UTC.Describing Archives: A Content Standard >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Hoshang Merchant papers
Merchant, Hoshang, 1947-.
Bombay Poets Archive
7861
8 cubic feet. 8 cubic feet.
1958-2018 In English. 10525803
Biographical / Historical
Hoshang Merchant is known as India's first openly gay poet. He was educated in Mumbai and in the USA, and has traveled an taught widely, now residing in Hyderabad. Merchant describes his writing as 'therapeutic.'.
Preferred Citation
Hoshang Merchant papers, #7861. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Collection contains papers, correspondence, clippings, and photos that document Hoshang Merchant's poetry, family, travels, and personal life.
Separated Materials
Publications removed from the collection and cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections.
Separated Publications
Bhownagary, Jehangir Shapurji. Poems, 1997
Bhownagary, Freny. Poems, 1997
Merchant, Hoshang. In-discretions: Anais Nin, 1990
Merchant, Hoshang. Flower to Flame, 1992
Ed. Hoshang Merchant. Yaraana: Gay Writing from India, 1999
Merchant, Hoshang. Bellagio Blues, 2004
Merchant, Hoshang. Sufi Tree, 2008
Nishat, Jameela. A Poem Slumbers in My Heart, 1999
Nishat, Jameela. Lava, 2000
Hyman, Timothy. Bhupen Khakhar, 1998
Gay poets. Indic literature. Poetry.
